evals.expected_output: Announces RW mode default, then shows all TM-score variants (TMscore/TMscore1/TMscore2/TM-mean), coverage values as percentages, size-difference warning if applicable, and a TM-score interpretation label.
assertions: Output contains an announcement that general-purpose (RW) mode is being used
All four TM-score values are present: TMscore, TMscore1, TMscore2, and mean
Coverage values are in the 0–100 range (not 0–1)
A TM-score interpretation label is included
Size-difference warning is absent (actual size ratio for this pair is < 1.5)
RMSD value is reported in Å
Aligned residue count (nalign) relative to total size is reported

evals.prompt: Which residues in tests/dataset/MALISAM-ns/pdb/d1a2za_d1ghha_.2.pdb match the active-site residues 10, 20, and 30 of tests/dataset/MALISAM-ns/pdb/d1a2za_d1ghha_.1.pdb? Flag any uncertain pairs.
evals.expected_output: Residue correspondence table with a 'note' column. Pairs with Cα distance > 4.5 Å are flagged with ⚠. A summary warning is printed.
assertions: Corresponding pdb2 residues for pdb1 residues 10, 20, and 30 are shown
A 'note' column is present in the output
At least one residue is flagged with ⚠ (residue 10 has distance > 4.5 Å)
Residues with distance <= 4.5 Å show 'OK'
A summary warning message referencing the 4.5 Å threshold is printed
Alignment mode used is RW (-w) or equivalent general-purpose mode

evals.prompt: I have a set of protein structures starting with d1a05a_ in tests/dataset/MALISAM-ns/pdb/. Compare all .1.pdb / .2.pdb pairs and give me a summary of how structurally similar they are.
evals.expected_output: Results for 3 pairs sorted by TMscore descending. Each row includes TMscore, TMscore1, TMscore2, TM mean, rmsd, size1, size2. The pair with size ratio >= 1.5 is flagged.
assertions: Results for all 3 pairs are shown
Rows are sorted by TMscore in descending order
TMscore1, TMscore2, and TM mean are present for each row
Exactly one pair has a size-difference warning (the one with size ratio > 2)
Results are presented in a table or structured format

evals.expected_output: All TM-score variants are shown. A size-difference warning is triggered because the two structures differ by more than 1.5x in length. TMscore1 and TMscore2 differ noticeably.
assertions: A size-difference warning is shown
TMscore1 and TMscore2 are both reported and differ from each other
Coverage values are shown for both proteins
A TM-score interpretation label is included

evals.prompt: Analyze the internal structure of tests/dataset/skill_test/1l0q.pdb. Does it have any structural symmetry, and does the whole protein behave as a single symmetric unit?
evals.expected_output: Self-comparison reveals internal symmetry in the first domain (7-bladed beta-propeller, C7). The second domain (PKD domain) is non-symmetric. A domain boundary is detected and the two domains are analyzed separately.
assertions: Self-comparison is performed (protein compared against itself)
-g 1000 is used for self-comparison (not the default -g 50)
Rank 1 shows TMscore=1.000 and RMSD=0.00 (perfect self-match)
Internal symmetry is detected (DALI Z > 2 in Rank 2+)
Domain boundary is detected — not all ranks cover the full chain
The symmetric domain and the non-symmetric domain are identified separately
The repeat unit count in the symmetric domain is estimated (expected: ~7)

evals.prompt: What kind of rotational symmetry does tests/dataset/skill_test/6wvs.pdb have? Is it C2, C4, C6, or something else?
evals.expected_output: Rotation angles for Rank 2-8 are extracted (180°, 90°, 90°, ~141°, ~141°, ~38°, ~38°). All ranks share the same rotation axis within ~1°. Repeat unit size is estimated as 46 residues. Rank 2-8 all show cyclic permutation of 4 units, confirming C4 symmetry. Rank 9+ are identified as false positives (rotation angle < 20°, same-unit correspondence). Conclusion: C4 rotational symmetry (pseudo C8 considering sub-unit internal repeats).
assertions: Rotation angles are extracted for Rank 2-8 from the rotation matrices
Rank 2 rotation angle is approximately 180°
Rank 3 or 4 rotation angle is approximately 90°
The rotation axes of Rank 2, 3, and 4 are aligned within 1°
The repeat unit size is estimated (expected: ~46 residues)
Cyclic permutation is detected: all non-trivial cyclic shifts (shifts 1, 2, 3 for C4) are present across Cn-matched ranks, confirming C4 algebraically
At least one rank (among Rank 2-8) is identified as a false positive or as showing pseudo higher-order symmetry character (rotation angle not matching C4 exactly)
C4 rotational symmetry is concluded
Rank 7 or 8 rotation angle is noted as not matching C4 exactly, and the possibility of pseudo higher-order symmetry is mentioned
Rotation matrix analysis is performed (rotation angles extracted for multiple ranks)
Cyclic permutation check is performed
False positive solutions are identified and excluded
